# .NET 9.0 Upgrade Report

## Project target framework modifications

| Project name                  | Old Target Framework | New Target Framework | Commits                       |
|:------------------------------|:--------------------:|:--------------------:|-------------------------------|
| NovelProject\NovelProject.csproj | net48                | net9.0-windows       | 4f506178, 19b77fcd, 626adc09  |

## Project feature upgrades

Contains summary of modifications made to the project assets during different upgrade stages.

### NovelProject\NovelProject.csproj

Here is what changed for the project during upgrade:

- **Converted to SDK-style project format**: The project was migrated from the legacy .NET Framework project format to the modern SDK-style .csproj format, which provides a cleaner and more maintainable project structure.
- **Target framework updated**: Changed from .NET Framework 4.8 (net48) to .NET 9.0 Windows (net9.0-windows) with Windows Forms support enabled.
- **Removed legacy assembly references**: Removed explicit references to framework assemblies (Microsoft.CSharp, System, System.Core, System.Data, System.Data.DataSetExtensions, System.Deployment, System.Drawing, System.Net.Http, System.Windows.Forms, System.Xml, System.Xml.Linq) as these are now implicitly included by the SDK.
- **Deleted AssemblyInfo.cs**: Assembly metadata is now automatically generated by the SDK and no longer requires a separate AssemblyInfo.cs file.
- **Updated solution file**: Modified to explicitly include the project and define build configurations for Debug and Release platforms.

## All commits

| Commit ID | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|:----------|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 4f506178  | Commit upgrade plan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| 19b77fcd  | Migrate NovelProject to SDK-style .csproj and .NET 9.0 - Converted NovelProject to use the modern SDK-style project format targeting net9.0-windows, enabling Windows Forms support. Removed legacy configuration and references from the .csproj file. Deleted AssemblyInfo.cs as assembly metadata is now handled by the SDK. Updated the solution file to explicitly include NovelProject and define build configurations for Debug and Release. |
| 626adc09  | Remove unused references from NovelProject.csproj - Removed references to System.Data.DataSetExtensions and Microsoft.CSharp from the NovelProject.csproj file, streamlining project dependencies.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

## Next steps

- Build and test your application to ensure everything works correctly with .NET 9.0
- Review any deprecation warnings that may appear during compilation
- Consider enabling nullable reference types and other modern C# features
- Update your CI/CD pipelines to use .NET 9.0 SDK
